summary.intent: Address 6 Codex review findings on the DateTimePicker component implementation, then sync the spec and push the fixes.
summary.outcome: All 6 findings fixed (calendar-aware date assembly, canonical ISO hidden input, trigger/clear button types, readonly guard, out-of-range guard), 125 tests passing with regenerated snapshots, spec updated, and changes committed and pushed.

repo: DateTimePicker uses a configured calendar for date assembly — must use CalendarDate::new with the component's calendar, not a hardcoded ISO calendar.
assemble_date follows a canonical pattern established in DateField; new date components should mirror it exactly.
DateTimePicker's readonly semantics differ from DatePicker: readonly blocks opening the popover entirely, so disabling the trigger under readonly is correct and per spec §3.1.
Trigger and clear buttons must carry explicit type="button" to avoid form-submission side effects — this is a sibling convention from DatePicker/DateField.
format_iso8601 must produce canonical ISO date strings for the hidden input value.
CalendarSelectDate transition arm requires an out-of-range guard consistent with DatePicker's pattern.
xci-fast is the pre-push gate (10 CI checks); snapshots must be regenerated and reviewed before it is run.
Spec lives at spec/components/date-time/date-time-picker.md and is validated with a dedicated lint step.

code.finding: assemble_date was fixed to use CalendarDate::new with the configured calendar and CalendarDateFields for field extraction, mirroring DateField's canonical pattern.
format_iso8601 updated to produce canonical ISO date strings for the hidden input.
Trigger attrs updated: disabled when readonly (not just aria-disabled), and type="button" added.
CalendarSelectDate transition arm now includes an out-of-range guard before committing the selected date.
Tests reference date_value via borrow (&context.date_value) not by move, because Context holds it behind a shared reference.

workflow: Codex review results are polled via a background task; verdict comes back with per-finding 👍/👀 signals.
Snapshot tests for HTML attribute changes must be regenerated (UPDATE_EXPECT or equivalent) and the diffs reviewed before accepting.
Spec must be updated in the same commit as implementation changes to keep them in sync.
Spec has a dedicated validation step that should be run before committing.

friction: Initial test run failed because date_value was moved out of a borrowed Context — required switching to a borrow, which was a minor but blocking compile error.
Multiple rounds of Read → Edit were needed on mod.rs because the file is large and each fix required re-reading a different section to get the correct old_string context.
